title: Find out if you need a work permit - Canada.ca
content:
## Find out
          what type of work permit you need
Get the right instructions you need to apply.
Check the list of options above again. If none of the other options describe you, select “other”.
- the main (principal) applicant
A temporary resident permit is only granted in exceptional circumstances to a person who is inadmissible but has a reason to travel to Canada.
A temporary resident permit is not the same as
- a visitor visa (temporary resident visa)
You’re a protected person if either
- the Immigration and Refugee Board (IRB) has found that you’re a Convention refugee, or a person in need of protection
- a Convention refugee, or
- a person in need of protection
- we have approved your application for a pre-removal risk assessment (PRRA)
Select this option if the other options don’t apply to you.
If you’re a foreign national looking to become a temporary worker, you might be
- outside Canada (immigrants from any country can apply for a work permit)
- in Canada while on a study permit
- in Canada working legally without a work permit

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
